content:
# Acts Chapter 2 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 226-227 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_002_page_001.jpg, ACT_002_page_002.jpg

---

**1** And in the being-fulfilled of-the day of-the Pentecost, they-were all together upon the same [place].

**2** And there-came suddenly out-of the heaven a sound as-of a violent wind being-borne, and it-filled the whole house where they-were sitting.

**3** And there-appeared to-them tongues being-distributed as of-fire, and it-sat upon each one of-them.

**4** And they-were all filled of-[the] Holy Spirit, and began to-speak with-other tongues, as the Spirit was-giving to-them to-utter-forth.

**5** Now there-were in Jerusalem dwelling Jews, devout men, from every nation of-those under the heaven.

**6** And this sound having-come, the multitude came-together and was-confused, because they-were-hearing each one in-the own dialect of-them [them] speaking.

**7** And they-were-amazed and were-marveling, saying, "Behold, are not all these the-[ones] speaking Galileans?

**8** And how are-we hearing each [one] in-the own dialect of-us in which we-were-born?

**9** Parthians and Medes and Elamites, and the-[ones] dwelling [in] Mesopotamia, Judea both and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,

**10** Phrygia both and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of-Libya the [one] toward Cyrene, and the sojourning Romans,

**11** Jews both and proselytes, Cretans and Arabians, we-hear them speaking in-the our tongues the great-[works] of-God."

**12** And they-were all amazed and were-perplexed, one to another saying, "What does this wish to-be?"

**13** But others mocking were-saying that "Of-sweet-wine they-are having-been-filled."

**14** But Peter, having-stood-up with the eleven, lifted-up the voice of-him and uttered-forth to-them, "Men, Jews, and all the-[ones] dwelling [in] Jerusalem, let this be-known to-you, and give-ear to-the words of-me.

**15** For not, as you suppose, these are-drunk; for it-is [the] third hour of-the day;

**16** but this is the-[thing] having-been-spoken through the prophet Joel:

**17** 'And it-shall-be in the last days, says God, I-will-pour-out from the Spirit of-me upon all flesh, and your sons and your daughters shall-prophesy, and your young-men shall-see visions, and your elders with-dreams shall-dream;

**18** and indeed upon the male-servants of-me and upon the female-servants of-me in those days I-will-pour-out from the Spirit of-me, and they-shall-prophesy.

**19** And I-will-give wonders in the heaven above, and signs upon the earth below, blood and fire and vapor of-smoke.

**20** The sun shall-be-turned into darkness and the moon into blood, before the coming of-the day of-[the] Lord, the great and manifest.

**21** And it-shall-be, everyone who if should-call-upon the name of-[the] Lord shall-be-saved.'

**22** Men, Israelites, hear these words: Jesus the Nazorean, a man having-been-demonstrated from God unto you by-powers and wonders and signs, which God did through him in [the] midst of-you, as you-yourselves know --

**23** this-[one], by-the having-been-determined counsel and foreknowledge of-God, [being] given-up, through [the] hand of-lawless-[ones] having-fastened-up, you-killed;

**24** whom God raised-up, having-loosed the pains of-death, because it-was not possible [for] him to-be-held by it.

**25** For David says unto him, 'I-was-seeing the Lord before me continually, because he-is at my right-[hand], that I-should not be-shaken.

**26** On-account-of this my heart was-gladdened and my tongue rejoiced, and-yet also my flesh shall-dwell upon hope;

**27** because you-will not abandon the soul of-me into Hades, nor will-you-give the Holy-[One] of-you to-see corruption.

**28** You-made-known to-me [the] ways of-life; you-will-fill me of-gladness with the face of-you.'

**29** Men, brothers, it-is-permitted to-speak with boldness to you concerning the patriarch David, that both he-died and was-buried, and the tomb of-him is among us until this day.

**30** A prophet therefore being, and knowing that with-an-oath God swore to-him, out-of [the] fruit of-the loins of-him to-seat upon the throne of-him,

**31** having-foreseen he-spoke concerning the resurrection of-the Christ, that neither was-he-abandoned into Hades, nor did the flesh of-him see corruption.

**32** This Jesus God raised-up, of-which we all are witnesses.

**33** Therefore by-the right-[hand] of-God having-been-exalted, and the promise of-the Holy Spirit having-received from the Father, he-poured-out this which you both see and hear.

**34** For David did not ascend into the heavens, but he-says himself, 'The Lord said to-the Lord of-me, "Sit at my right-[hand],

**35** until I-should-place the enemies of-you [as] a footstool of-the feet of-you."'

**36** Assuredly therefore let-know, all [the] house of-Israel, that both Lord and Christ God made him, this Jesus whom you crucified."

**37** And having-heard, they-were-pierced to-the heart, and-they said to Peter and the rest of-the apostles, "What shall-we-do, men, brothers?"

**38** And Peter [said] to them, "Repent, and let-be-baptized each of-you upon the name of-Jesus Christ unto forgiveness of-sins, and you-shall-receive the gift of-the Holy Spirit.

**39** For to-you is the promise, and to-the children of-you, and to-all the-[ones] unto far-[distance], as-many-as the Lord our God should-call-to [himself]."

**40** And with-other words more he-was-solemnly-testifying and was-exhorting them, saying, "Be-saved from this crooked generation."

**41** Those indeed therefore having-received the word of-him were-baptized, and there-were-added in that day souls about three thousand.

**42** And they-were persevering in-the teaching of-the apostles and in-the fellowship, in-the breaking of-the bread and in-the prayers.

**43** And there-came upon every soul fear, and many wonders and signs through the apostles were-coming-to-pass.

**44** And all the-[ones] having-believed were upon the same [place], and were-having all-things common;

**45** and the possessions and the properties they-were-selling, and were-distributing them to-all, as anyone was-having need.

**46** Daily both persevering with-one-accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house [to house], they-were-sharing food in gladness and simplicity of-heart,

**47** praising God and having favor toward all the people. And the Lord was-adding the-[ones] being-saved daily upon the same [place].

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript and Textual Observations

1. **Source Folios:** Translated from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century), Quire 77, pages visible on PDF pages 226-227. The text of Acts 2 begins in column 3 of page 226 (following Acts 1:26) and continues through column 2 of page 227, where Acts 3 begins.

2. **v. 1 -- "being-fulfilled" (συμπληρουσθαι):** Sinaiticus reads the present passive infinitive in the genitive articular construction (ΕΝ ΤΩ ΣΥΜΠΛΗΡΟΥΣΘΑΙ). This is a temporal construction: "in the being-filled-up" of the day. Some later manuscripts read a simpler form. Preserved literally here.

3. **v. 3 -- "tongues being-distributed" (γλωσσαι διαμεριζομεναι):** The participle is middle/passive, indicating tongues "distributing themselves" or "being distributed." The "as of fire" (ωσει πυρος) is a comparison, not an identification -- these were not literal fire but appeared fire-like.

4. **v. 4 -- "to-utter-forth" (αποφθεγγεσθαι):** This verb carries the sense of inspired or prophetic speech, stronger than ordinary "speaking." It is used in the LXX for prophetic utterance.

5. **v. 17 -- "in the last days" (εν ταις εσχαταις ημεραις):** Sinaiticus reads "in the last days" (ΕΣΧΑΤΑΙΣ ΗΜΕΡΑΙΣ). The Hebrew of Joel 3:1 [2:28 in English versification] reads "after these things" (אחרי כן). Peter's quotation, following the LXX, shifts this to an eschatological frame.

6. **v. 20 -- "manifest" (επιφανη):** Sinaiticus clearly reads ΕΠΙΦΑΝΗ ("manifest/splendid"). The Hebrew of Joel has "terrible/fearful" (נורא). The LXX translator rendered this differently. Some later Greek manuscripts harmonize toward "terrible" (επιφοβον). Sinaiticus preserves the LXX reading.

7. **v. 23 -- "having-fastened-up" (προσπηξαντες):** Literally "having-nailed-to." This is an aorist active participle, describing the act of crucifixion. Sinaiticus reads ΠΡΟΣΠΗΞΑΝΤΕΣ. Some manuscripts read a different verb here.

8. **v. 27 -- "Hades" (αδην):** Transliterated from Greek, itself a rendering of Hebrew "Sheol" (שאול). This is the realm of the dead, not "hell" in the later theological sense. Preserved as transliteration per EMET protocol.

9. **v. 30 -- "out-of [the] fruit of-the loins of-him":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΚ ΚΑΡΠΟΥ ΤΗΣ ΟΣΦΥΟΣ ΑΥΤΟΥ. Some later manuscripts add "according to the flesh, to raise up the Christ" -- an expansion not present in Sinaiticus. The shorter reading is preserved here as it appears in the manuscript.

10. **v. 31 -- "neither was-he-abandoned into Hades, nor did the flesh of-him see corruption":** Sinaiticus reads "the flesh of him" (Η ΣΑΡΞ ΑΥΤΟΥ) where some manuscripts read "his soul" (η ψυχη αυτου) for the first clause. This is a significant variant. Sinaiticus's reading in the second clause emphasizes bodily resurrection.

11. **v. 33 -- "having-received" (λαβων):** The subject is Jesus -- having been exalted to the right hand of God and having received the promise of the Holy Spirit from the Father, he (Jesus) poured out this. The grammar makes Christ the agent of the Spirit's outpouring.

12. **v. 38 -- "upon the name" (επι τω ονοματι):** Sinaiticus reads ΕΠΙ with the dative, meaning "upon/on the basis of the name." Other manuscripts read ΕΝ ("in") or ΕΙΣ ("into"). The preposition affects the theological sense: "upon" suggests "on the authority of" rather than a baptismal formula.

13. **v. 41 -- "about three thousand":** Sinaiticus reads ΩΣΕΙ ΤΡΙΣΧΙΛΙΑΙ ("about three thousand"). The approximation is original to the text.

14. **v. 47 -- "upon the same [place]" (επι το αυτο):** This phrase ΕΠΙ ΤΟ ΑΥΤΟ appears at the end of v. 47 in Sinaiticus, and its attachment is debated -- some traditions connect it to the beginning of 3:1 instead. In Sinaiticus, it appears to conclude chapter 2. The phrase literally means "upon the same" and implies togetherness or co-location.

15. **General note on the Joel quotation (vv. 17-21):** Peter's sermon quotes Joel 3:1-5 (LXX) / Joel 2:28-32 (MT). The quotation in Sinaiticus follows the LXX closely, with minor variations. The shift from Joel's "after these things" to "in the last days" is present already in the LXX tradition that Sinaiticus reflects.

16. **Psalm 16 quotation (vv. 25-28):** The quotation from Psalm 15:8-11 (LXX) / Psalm 16:8-11 (MT) is rendered faithfully. Sinaiticus preserves the LXX text form.

17. **Psalm 110 quotation (vv. 34-35):** From Psalm 109:1 (LXX) / Psalm 110:1 (MT). The double use of ΚΥΡΙΟΣ -- "The Lord said to the Lord of me" -- reflects the Hebrew where YHWH speaks to Adonai.

-- EMET
